patches.find: Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's 69-confirmed / 94.5%-accuracy scorecard alongside empirically tested claims like Schumann frequency and SAA field strength — pricing theological convergence at par with data-grounded physics. Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's confirmed-prediction scorecard alongside empirically testable WINs — the scorecard accuracy figure does not distinguish between testable predictions and unfalsifiable theological reframings.
patches.replace: Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's 69-confirmed / 94.5%-accuracy scorecard alongside empirically tested claims like Schumann frequency and SAA field strength — the scorecard accuracy figure does not distinguish between testable predictions and unfalsifiable theological reframings, pricing theological convergence at par with data-grounded physics.
patches.rationale: Two consecutive near-identical sentences both starting 'Nevertheless, WIN-031...' introduced by EXP-216 integration that added the new sentence without removing the old one. Merged into a single sentence combining the strongest framing from each.

patches.find: 1 Enoch 17–18 then fills in a visionary cosmic geography with seven sacred mountains in a visionary cosmic geography.
patches.replace: 1 Enoch 17–18 then fills in this visionary cosmic geography with seven sacred mountains.
patches.rationale: Phrase 'visionary cosmic geography' appeared twice in the same sentence — introduced by commit 6b81df2 while removing the inaccurate 'at the earth's center' phrase. Lowest-touch fix: drop the duplicate rider, change 'a' to 'this' for flow.

patches.find: this is a consequence of geographic orientation, not dome geometry.
patches.replace: this is a consequence of geographic orientation, not dome geometry. This cartographic convergence is equally compatible with a spherical earth, on which north points toward a geographic pole, or with any other axially-symmetric geometry — so the texts' directional usage distinguishes no cosmology in particular.
patches.rationale: c5 carry-forward (ISS-1409): add preemptive clause closing the 'flat-earth cartographic orientation' defender wedge. The following sentence already makes a symmetry-class argument, but does not explicitly name 'spherical earth' — adding this prevents the strongest dome-defender reframe.

patches.find: ~1,000× greater sensitivity than the Mohe spring gravimeter.
patches.replace: roughly 100–500× greater sensitivity than the Mohe spring gravimeter (modern SG noise floors reach ~0.01 μGal vs. ~1–5 μGal for spring gravimeters, with the exact ratio depending on integration time and drift correction).
patches.rationale: ~1,000× was at the upper end of defensible; honest range is 100–500×. Dome defender would exploit the overstatement. Cite the noise-floor basis so the figure is bounded by data rather than rhetorical.

patches.find: (c) if location-dependent: the model avoids contradiction by asserting different sites respond differently, but provides no advance specification of which sites should show effects — rendering any future null result unfalsifiable.
patches.replace: (c) if location-dependent: the model avoids contradiction by asserting different sites respond differently, but provides no advance specification of which sites should show effects — rendering any future null result unfalsifiable. A defender might attribute the difference to subsurface conductivity (basement granite at Mohe vs. Devonian carbonate at Membach), but the dome's published WIN list and monitor.py contain no subsurface-conductivity term, no per-site coupling tensor, and no advance prediction tying expected response to any site-specific geophysical parameter. The escape is post-hoc rationalization, not a model output.
patches.rationale: Close the strongest defender rebuttal: a two-component model with site-dependent coupling (granite vs. carbonate). Dome has no published formula for this, so the escape is demonstrably post-hoc.

patches.find: Van Camp et al., 2001) as additional confirmation.
patches.replace: Van Camp et al., 2001 — the dome cites 'Van Camp 1999' (the eclipse year); the paper appeared in 2001, five years after the eclipse, making the dome's 1999 attribution impossible to reconcile with publication history) as additional confirmation.
patches.rationale: Surface the dome's misdated citation on the WIN-013 panel itself. The year discrepancy is documented in the citation check but not visible to readers expanding Evidence.

patches.find: The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.) that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit
patches.replace: The confirming value comes from the Van Camp et al. 2001 Membach SG eclipse measurement (DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637), publicly indexed since 2001 — five years before the dome model existed — and was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit
patches.rationale: Replace generic boilerplate dataset list with the actual source (Van Camp 2001). WIN-013's post-hoc paragraph was using the template's generic list rather than naming the specific Membach SG paper.

EXP-244 cluster canonicalization missed detail_extra. This is the single residual Chapman 1933 reference across WIN-010/025/068. Replace with the correct 1919 Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 218 canonical citation and update href from generic Wikipedia bio to Royal Society DOI.

patches.find: by Yamazaki & Maute (2017, Space Science Reviews 206:299–405). During a solar eclipse
patches.replace: by Yamazaki & Maute (2017, Space Science Reviews 206:299–405). During a solar eclipse
patches.rationale: First mention of Y&M 2017 in detail_evidence was bare (no hyperlink). Second mention further down already has the DOI link. Add the same link to the first mention for citation completeness and click-through verifiability.
